text:
There once was an old storyteller named Grumpius who lived alone in a gloomy wooden cottage. Every evening, he would gather around his flickering hearth, clutching his worn leather journal, and spin tales so enchanting they sent listeners into a joyful slumber.

One autumn twilight, a youth named Timmy knocked at Grumpius' door. Timmy was a wandering minstrel, searching for inspiration for his songs. Grumpius invited him in, and they settled down on worn wool rugs.

"Tell me your wildest tale," Timmy urged.

With a sigh, Grumpius began to weave words into a tapestry of adventure.

"The Legend of Yarrowholme," he started. "Long ago, in a land full of magic and mystery, there was an ancient cherry blossom tree. This tree granted wishes, but with a twist—once a wish was spoken, petals of a rare, shimmering plum would fall, sealing the magic."

Timmy
